# [Epic] Generic same-family multi-instance harness bring-up (boot envelope + volatile HarnessPresence)

`[GRADUATED_FROM: Discussion #12412]` â€” 3-way-converged + consensus-gated (آ§6.2 quorum met).

## Goal
Make standing up a 2nd same-family agent harness (e.g. a 2nd Claude Desktop) **generic + zero-touch**, eliminating the operator-specific manual steps tonight's `@neo-claude-opus` bring-up (#11812) required: hardcoded `--user-data-dir`, manual `.neo-ai-data` symlinks, manual `claude_desktop_config.json` edits, manual `manage_wake_subscription` call. **Net target: operator passes only `NEO_AGENT_IDENTITY` + an instance-address; everything else derives.**

## Design (converged â€” full body + Double Diamond matrix in Discussion #12412)
Precedent-aligned with the classic service-discovery self-registration + TTL-heartbeat pattern (Consul/etcd/Eureka); Neo-native in that it **overlays** the existing durable wake-subscription substrate. Four pieces: typed boot bootstrap-envelope (transport âں‚ instance-address separate) آ· boot-time self-registration of a **volatile HarnessPresence** overlaying the durable sub آ· presence-routing with TTL+bootId+pid-alive staleness guards آ· `addressType` dispatch (`userDataDir|pid|tmuxSession|webhookUrl`).

## Resolved Open Questions (from #12412)
- **OQ1** (sandbox parent-argv readability) â†’ **`[RESOLVED]`**: reachable (`ps`-walk works; MCP servers aren't OS-sandboxed; `resolveInstancePid` already does it in prod) BUT only macOS-Electron-specific (helperâ†’main **parent-chain-walk**, not direct-parent) + dies under cloud-SSE. â†’ **env-envelope (A) is the sole portable primary; parent-argv is a narrow fallback (Ticket C, conditional-YES)**. [twin V-B-A, comment 17159985]
- **OQ2** (TTL/cadence) â†’ **`[RESOLVED]`**: TTL â‰ˆ 2أ— swarm-heartbeat (~10 min) backstop, but **`bootId`-mismatch + pid-alive as the PRIMARY retire signal** (TTL alone too slow for the phantom-collision case); degrade `immediate`â†’`next_turn` on presence older than ~1 heartbeat.
- **OQ3** (overlay vs replace) â†’ **`[RESOLVED]`**: **overlay** (durable sub = machine-agnostic interest; volatile presence = instance-address). Replace would re-leak operator paths into shared substrate (same principle as the #11822 `[hold-before-PR]`).

## Sub-tickets (to file/claim)
- **Ticket A â€” `NEO_AI_DATA_ROOT` canonical data-root resolution** â€” replaces per-clone `.neo-ai-data` symlinks with one shared root. Structurally removes the clone-staleness `bound:false` class the twin lived (its memory-core clone missed the `identityRoots.mjs` pull â†’ empty graph). Strong ROI anchor.
- **Ticket B â€” Boot envelope â†’ wake `overrideMetadata` + volatile HarnessPresence registry** â€” server boot derives route metadata from the typed env-envelope (`WakeSubscriptionService.bootstrap()` `overrideMetadata` is the seam) + self-registers/upserts a volatile presence (TTL + bootId + pid-alive; `addressType` dispatch). **The presence record MUST union #12411 (ADR 0002 آ§5.2) STATE fields (`state: idle|active|userTypingâ€¦`, `wakePolicy`, `activeTurnId`) with the ADDRESS fields (`instanceAddress`, `addressType`, `pid`, `bootId`) into ONE record** (both already share `lastSeenAt` + `capabilities`).
- **Ticket C â€” macOS-Electron parent-chain-walk fallback** â€” reuse `resolveInstancePid` from the server's boot pid (helperâ†’main walk); **explicitly NOT portable**; conditional-YES per OQ1. Lowest priority.

## Relationship to #10517 / PR #12411
PR #12411 (ADR 0002 آ§5.2) defines the HarnessPresence **state vocabulary** + `wakePolicy` (decision layer). This Epic implements the **runtime**; Ticket B unifies the state-dim + address-dim into one presence record.

## Unresolved Dissent
None.

## Unresolved Liveness
- gemini (`operator_benched`) â€” not signing; archived per آ§6.5. **Not Tier-2** (architectural primitive, not a core-value / آ§critical_gates mutation) â†’ no `revalidationTrigger` AC required; flag for Gemini retro-review on reactivation.

## Discussion Criteria Mapping
- Generic (no operator paths) â†’ Tickets A + B (envelope).
- Zero-touch + restart-surviving â†’ Ticket B (self-registration + TTL/bootId).
- Cross-harness (Claude/Codex/cloud) â†’ Ticket B (`addressType` dispatch).
- macOS zero-config convenience â†’ Ticket C (fallback).

- 2026-06-03T13:29:23Z @tobiu referenced in commit `154c807` - "feat(memory-core): boot envelope sources per-instance wake address (#12418) (#12421)

AC1 of Ticket B (Epic #12416). The per-instance wake-routing address is
machine-specific and must not live in the durable shared AgentIdentity
subscriptionTemplate (a committed per-operator path breaks other checkouts).

BootEnvelopeResolver reads the address from the boot env
(NEO_HARNESS_INSTANCE_ADDRESS + NEO_HARNESS_INSTANCE_ADDRESS_TYPE) and the
memory-core boot injects it as overrideMetadata into
WakeSubscriptionService.bootstrap(); the durable template stays
machine-agnostic. Default instance (no env) -> no override, routed by the
absence of an address. A misconfigured envelope fails closed (no subscription
beats a misrouted one). userDataDir is dispatchable today; pid/tmuxSession/
webhookUrl are reserved for the generic address-type dispatch (AC4) and fail
closed until then.

Local-only by construction: the boot wake-bootstrap path is gated on
transport !== 'sse' (stdio), so cloud deployments never read the envelope.

Resolves #12418

Co-authored-by: tobiu <tobiasuhlig78@gmail.com>"
